Execution version  

**Confidentiality Agreement**  

---

**Parties**  

[Redacted] company number 3072505, having its registered office at [Redacted], New Zealand (the *Disclosing Party*)  
Email address: [Redacted]  

and  

**University of Auckland**, a university constituted under the University of Auckland Act 1961 and a tertiary education provider under the Education and Training Act 2020, of 22 Princes Street, Auckland, New Zealand (the *Receiving Party*)  
Email address: contracts@auckland.ac.nz  

---

**Background**  

In consideration of the Disclosing Party agreeing to make Confidential Information available to the Receiving Party, and its Representatives, the Receiving Party agrees to the terms contained in this confidentiality agreement (*Agreement*).  

---

**1. Definitions**  

1.1 The following definitions apply for the purposes of this Agreement:  

- **Applicable Law** means any law, regulation, order or request of any governmental, official or regulatory body (including the rules of any relevant financial market or stock exchange), or any court of competent jurisdiction.  

- **Approved Purpose** means the Disclosing Party’s participation in the Receiving Party’s Business Analytics Applied Project including the provision of workplace experience to the [Redacted].  

- **Confidential Information** means any Information in connection with the Disclosing Party or its Group which is made available by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party or its Representatives for the Approved Purpose whether before or after the date of this Agreement, and includes:  

  (a) the existence and the terms of this Agreement;  

  (b) the fact that Confidential Information has been or may be provided to the Receiving Party or its Representatives; and  

  (c) the existence, content or status of any discussions, negotiations or agreements connected with the Approved Purpose including any type of offer made by or on behalf of any party or Group member in connection with the Approved Purpose.  

  but does not include information which:  

  (a) is publicly available at the time of its disclosure under this Agreement; or  

---

*Template version December 2025*

Execution version
(b) becomes publicly available following disclosure under this Agreement (other than as a
result of disclosure contrary to the terms of this Agreement); or
(c) was lawfully in the possession of the Receiving Party prior to disclosure under this
Agreement (as can be demonstrated by written records or other reasonable evidence)
and is free of any restriction owed to the Disclosing Party, as to its use or disclosure; or
(d) following disclosure under this Agreement, becomes available to the Receiving Party
(as can be demonstrated by written records or other reasonable evidence) from a
source other than Disclosing Party, which source is not, to the knowledge of the
Receiving Party, bound by any obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party, in
relation to such information; or
(e) is independently developed by the Receiving Party following disclosure under this
Agreement without use of or reference to the Confidential Information.
Group means, in relation to a party, that party and its Related Companies.
Information means information in any form including writing, visual, machine-readable
form or orally and includes any copies of such information and any information, analyses,
compilations, notes, studies, memoranda or other documents derived from, containing or
reflecting such information.
Related Company has the meaning given to that term in section 2(3) of the Companies Act
1993, read as if reference to "company" in that subsection included any body corporate or
other entity, wherever incorporated.
Representative means in relation to a party, each member of that party’s Group and the
directors, officers, employees, contractors or advisers of that party or its Group.
Termination Date: is the earliest of the date:
(a) which is two years from the date of this Agreement:
(b) of execution of a definitive agreement in relation to the Approved Purpose; or
(c) the parties agree, in writing, that the obligations imposed by this Agreement,
cease.
1.2 For the purposes of this Agreement:
(a) where the context allows, a reference to a party means either or
University of Auckland (as the case may be); and
(b) the meaning of general words is not limited by specific examples introduced by includes,
including, for example, such as and similar expressions.
2. Undertakings in respect of Confidential Information
2.1 Subject to clause 3, the Receiving Party undertakes that it will:
(a) keep the Confidential Information secret and confidential and not disclose it to any person
other than its Representatives, in compliance with the terms of this Agreement, who
reasonably require it for the Approved Purpose;
(b) use the Confidential Information solely for the Approved Purpose in accordance with this
2
Template version December 2025

Execution version
Agreement and not for any other purpose;
(c) keep the Confidential Information secure and in such a way so as to prevent unauthorised
access by any third party;
(d) not make any copies of the Confidential Information or reproduce it in any form except for
the purpose of supplying the same to those to whom disclosure is permitted in accordance
with this Agreement; and
(e) promptly inform the Disclosing Party if it becomes aware that the Confidential Information
has been disclosed to (or accessed by) an unauthorised party.
2.2 Neither party may use the names, trademarks or logos of the other party or its personnel without
the other party’s prior written consent.
3. Permitted disclosure of Confidential Information
3.1 The provisions of clause 2 do not restrict any disclosure by the Receiving Party of the Confidential
Information to the extent that it is:
(a) required by Applicable Law provided that, so far as it is lawful and practical to do so prior to
such disclosure, the Receiving Party will promptly notify the Disclosing Party and will:
(i) provide reasonable assistance to enable the Disclosing Party (at the Disclosing Party’s
cost) to contest the disclosure or to seek any other appropriate remedy if applicable, and/or
(ii) consult with, and comply with the reasonable directions of, the Disclosing Party
regarding the nature, timing and content of the proposed disclosure; or
(b) consented to in writing by the Disclosing Party prior to such disclosure.
4. Compliance by Representatives
4.1. The Receiving Party undertakes to procure that its Representatives will observe the obligations
and undertakings contained in this Agreement as if each of its Representatives were a party to
this Agreement and agrees to be liable for any breach of such obligations and undertakings by
any of its Representatives.
5. Return or destruction of Confidential Information
5.1. The Receiving Party undertakes that, subject to clause 6, it will at its expense, promptly upon
receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party:
(a) return or destroy (at its option) all of the Confidential Information provided to it and its
Representatives pursuant to this Agreement, without keeping any copies of any of the
Confidential Information;
(b) procure the destruction of all analyses, compilations, notes, studies, memoranda and other
documents prepared by it and by each of its Representatives to the extent that the same
contain, reflect or derive from any of the Confidential Information; and
(c) so far as it is practicable to do so (but, in any event, without prejudice to the obligations of
confidentiality contained in this Agreement), expunge any of the Confidential Information
from any readily accessible computer, server, word processor or other device used by it and
3
Template version December 2025

Execution version
each of its Representatives. For the avoidance of doubt, this does not require the deletion
of copies of the Confidential Information made during routine backup processes.
5.2 At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party undertakes to supply written
confirmation confirming that the requirements of clause 5.1 have been fully complied with.
6. Exception to return or destruction of Confidential Information
6.1. The obligation to return, destroy or expunge the Confidential Information will not apply to the
Receiving Party to the extent that:
(a) the Confidential Information is contained in internal board or committee papers or minutes:
(b) it is required to retain the Confidential Information in order to comply with any Applicable
Law; or
(c) in the case of a Representative who is a professional adviser, it is required to retain the
Confidential Information for professional standards or indemnity insurance requirements.
6.2 Information retained under clause 6.1 must not be used for any purpose other than for the
record-keeping purposes set out above and remains subject to the confidentiality obligations
set out in this Agreement.
7. Ownership
7.1. Except as may be set out in the terms of any definitive agreements entered into in connection
with the Approved Purpose, Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing
Party and the disclosure of any Confidential Information will not confer on the Receiving Party
any rights (including any intellectual property rights) over the disclosed Confidential Information
whatsoever.
7.2. The Receiving Party undertakes that it will not assert any rights in respect of, or contest the
Disclosing Party’s ownership of, the Confidential Information.
8. No liability for Confidential Information
8.1. The Confidential Information is provided on the basis that the Receiving Party will be responsible
for its own independent assessment of that information.
8.2 Except as may be set out in the terms of any definitive agreements entered into in connection
with the Approved Purpose, the Disclosing Party:
(a) has no responsibility for, or makes any representation, express or implied, or gives any
warranty or assumes or acknowledges any liability with respect to, the accuracy, adequacy
or completeness of the Confidential Information or any oral communication in connection
with the Confidential Information; or as to materiality of any Confidential Information in
the context of the Approved Purpose; or
(b) is not obliged to update any such information or to correct inaccuracies in such information;
or
(c) is not obliged to disclose any kind or quantity of information to the Receiving Party.
4
Template version December 2025

Execution version
8.3 The maximum aggregate liability of the Receiving Party to the Disclosing Party under or in
connection with this Agreement is limited to .
9. Personal Information
9.1. To the extent any personal information about identifiable individuals is made available in
connection with the Approved Purpose, then the Receiving Party and its Representatives will
comply with the Privacy Act 2020 (NZ), and all other Applicable Laws which regulate the
collection, storage, use and/or disclosure of personal information.
10. Conduct of Process
10.1. Except as may be set out in the terms of any definitive agreements entered into in connection
with the Approved Purpose, each party acknowledges that any party may in its sole and absolute
discretion terminate discussions and negotiations in relation to the Approved Purpose at any
time.
11. Duration
11.1. The obligations set out in this Agreement will, subject to clause 11.2, continue in full force and
effect from the date of this Agreement and will end on the Termination Date.
11.2 Notwithstanding clause 11.1, the Receiving Party agrees that the obligations under clauses 5, 6,
7, 8 and 9 survive termination of this Agreement and will continue for a period of 5 years from
the Termination Date.
12. Notices
12.1. Every notice or other communication for the purposes of this Agreement will be in writing and
delivered by:
(a) delivery to the registered office or other notified physical address of the relevant party,
which is deemed to be received at the time of delivery; or
(b) sending it by email to the email address of the relevant party, which is deemed to be
received, unless the sender receives a failed delivery notice, at time of transmission if sent
between the hours of 9am and 5pm (local time) on a local working day, and if not, at 9am
(local time) on the next local working day. For this purpose, "local time" is the time in the
place of receipt of the notice, and a "local working day" is a normal working day in that place.
13. Amendments
13.1. No (a) amendment to this Agreement, (b) agreement between the parties for the purpose of, or
referred to in, this Agreement, or (c) consent or approval for the purposes of, or referred to in,
this Agreement, is effective unless it is in writing and signed (if sub-clause (a) or (b) apply) by the
parties or (if sub-clause (c) applies) the party required to give the consent or approval.
14. Remedies
14.1. Without prejudice to any other rights or remedies that a party may have, each party
acknowledges and agrees that damages may not be an adequate remedy for any breach of the
provisions of this Agreement and that the persons entitled to the benefit of this agreement may
be entitled to seek the remedies of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief
for any threatened or actual breach of any such provision and that no proof of special damages
5
Template version December 2025

```
Execution version

will be necessary for the enforcement by such persons of their rights under this Agreement. Nothing contained in this clause will, however, be construed as a waiver by a party of any other rights including the right to seek damages for a breach of the provisions of this Agreement.

15. Waiver

15.1. No failure or delay by any party in exercising any of its rights under this Agreement will operate as a waiver of such party’s rights, nor will any single or partial exercise preclude any other further exercise of such rights.

16. Assignment

16.1. No party may assign or transfer its rights under this Agreement in whole or in part to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party.

17. Severability

17.1. Each provision of this Agreement is separate and severable and enforceable accordingly. If any provision or part of a provision is found to be void, invalid or otherwise unenforceable, the remaining part of the provisions will continue in full force and effect.

18. Counterparts

18.1. This Agreement may be signed in counterparts and all counterparts taken together constitute one document.

19. Entire Agreement

19.1. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes and cancels any previous agreement, understanding or arrangement whether written or oral.

20. Governing Law

20.1. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of New Zealand. Each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts exercising jurisdiction in New Zealand in relation to all matters arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

Signed by:

[Redacted] by:                                Signed for and on behalf of the University of Auckland by:

_________________________                  _________________________
Authorised signatory                        Authorised signatory

_________________________                  _________________________
Full name of authorised signatory          Full name of authorised signatory

Date: ________________________

Template version December 2025
```
